matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: rs7134216 (BLTP3B/UHRF1BP1L MYP3-region marker) (UHRF1BP1L intronic MYP3 high-grade myopia association marker)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BLTP3B/UHRF1BP1L membrane-trafficking and MYP3-locus thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade marker tied to bridge-like lipid transfer, early endosome-to-Golgi traffic, or cohort-level high-grade myopia association context. This is an exploratory locus signal, not a diagnostic myopia prediction or a proven BLTP3B protein-function call.

variant_prediction_rules.common_name: UHRF1BP1L intronic MYP3 high-grade myopia association marker
variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed BLTP3B/UHRF1BP1L rs7134216 suggests a MYP3 high-grade myopia locus thesis: the sample matched an intronic marker reported in association mapping and replicated for quantitative spherical refractive error in an independent high-grade myopia cohort. Keep the prediction cohort-level and research-oriented because the bundled evidence does not establish deterministic myopia risk or a direct BLTP3B protein mechanism.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs7134216 (BLTP3B/UHRF1BP1L MYP3-region marker) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the BLTP3B prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Intronic marker in the legacy UHRF1BP1L/BLTP3B locus used in high-grade myopia association mapping.

evidence.label: PMCID PMC3621505: rs7134216 in UHRF1BP1L and MYP3 association mapping
NCBI Gene 23074: BLTP3B coordinates and aliases

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BLTP3B/UHRF1BP1L membrane-trafficking and MYP3-locus thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade marker tied to bridge-like lipid transfer, early endosome-to-Golgi traffic, or cohort-level high-grade myopia association context. This is an exploratory locus signal, not a diagnostic myopia prediction or a proven BLTP3B protein-function call. An observed BLTP3B variant places this sample in the curated BLTP3B research context. The local BLTP3B knowledge base is membrane-trafficking and research-association oriented. It focuses on lipid-transfer/endosome-Golgi biology and on MYP3 high-grade myopia locus evidence for the former UHRF1BP1L symbol, not on a validated high-penetrance diagnostic BLTP3B variant panel. Most interpretable inherited BLTP3B signals in this bundle are research association markers rather than diagnostic pathogenic variants.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Early endosome-to-Golgi traffic, GARP/syntaxin-6-associated sorting, and bulk lipid-transfer biology.
High-grade myopia and MYP3 chromosome 12q21-q23 association-mapping studies.
Membrane-contact-site and VPS13/ATG2-like bridge lipid transport research.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a BLTP3B variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BLTP3B/UHRF1BP1L membrane-trafficking and MYP3-locus thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade marker tied to bridge-like lipid transfer, early endosome-to-Golgi traffic, or cohort-level high-grade myopia association context. This is an exploratory locus signal, not a diagnostic myopia prediction or a proven BLTP3B protein-function call. BLTP3B is a reverse-strand gene and the bundled EPIC slice intentionally includes the 1 kb downstream promoter/TSS window. Many manifest rows still use the legacy UHRF1BP1L annotation; the curated whitelist therefore anchors interpretation by probe ID around the BLTP3B TSS. Treat beta values as promoter-proximal regulatory context, not as a validated BLTP3B disease methylation assay. The local BLTP3B knowledge base is membrane-trafficking and research-association oriented. It focuses on lipid-transfer/endosome-Golgi biology and on MYP3 high-grade myopia locus evidence for the former UHRF1BP1L symbol, not on a validated high-penetrance diagnostic BLTP3B variant panel. Most interpretable inherited BLTP3B signals in this bundle are research association markers rather than diagnostic pathogenic variants.
